title: Rubber Duck Titration Lab
description: A chemistry lab bench where the user performs a multi-step titration experiment using a coffee mug as the reaction vessel, a water bottle as the reagent dispenser, and a rubber duck as a pH indicator that changes color based on solution acidity. The user must carefully add reagents, stir, record measurements, and use undo/redo to correct mistakes while conserving total liquid volume across containers.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with GLTFLoader and OrbitControls from CDN. Set up a chemistry lab scene on a flat table surface with soft directional and ambient lighting, and a light gray background. Load coffeeMug.glb on the left as the reaction vessel, WaterBottle.glb on the right as the reagent dispenser, and Duck.glb floating inside the mug as a pH indicator. Display a HUD overlay showing: current pH (starts at 2.0, target 7.0), mug liquid volume (starts 200mL), bottle remaining volume (starts 300mL), and step counter. Implement these interactions: (1) Click the water bottle to select it, then click the mug to dispense 25mL of base reagent, reducing bottle volume and increasing mug volume (conservation law: total liquid always equals 500mL). (2) Each addition shifts pH toward 7.0 using a realistic titration curve with a steep jump near equivalence point. (3) The duck changes color smoothly: red at pH<4, orange at pH 4-5, yellow at pH 5-6, green at pH 6.5-7.5, blue at pH>8, using material emissive color interpolation. (4) Click the duck to stir the solution, triggering a bobbing animation and finalizing the pH reading. (5) A measurement log panel on the right records each step with pH and volumes. (6) Implement undo (Ctrl+Z) and redo (Ctrl+Y) that fully reverse or reapply any dispensing or stirring action, restoring all state including duck color, volumes, and pH. (7) If the user overshoots past pH 8, the duck turns blue and a warning appears. (8) A reset button restores initial state. (9) A submit button checks if pH is between 6.8 and 7.2 and displays success or failure. The mug should have a translucent liquid plane inside that rises with volume and tints based on pH. The water bottle should tilt during pour with a short animation. Require 10-14 deliberate steps including pours, stirs, at least one undo, and final submission to complete successfully.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  currentPH: number,         // current pH of solution in mug, starts at 2.0, target is 7.0
  mugLiquidVolume: number,         // volume of liquid in mug in mL, starts at 200
  bottleRemainingVolume: number,         // volume remaining in water bottle in mL, starts at 300
  stepCounter: number,         // count of reagent additions performed, starts at 0
  bottleSelected: boolean,         // whether water bottle is currently selected for dispensing
  totalDispensedVolume: number,         // cumulative volume dispensed from bottle in mL
  stirCount: number,         // number of times duck has been clicked to stir solution
  duckColorHue: number,         // current hue value (0-360) of duck's emissive color based on pH
  isStirring: boolean,         // whether duck is currently performing bobbing animation
  measurementLogEntries: number,         // count of pH measurements recorded in log panel
  equivalencePointReached: boolean,         // whether pH has reached target range 6.5-7.5
}
